---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Lester"
date: "2023-03-03"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(UpSetR)
library(tidyverse)
```

## Load data
```{r cars}
files <- list.files("C:/Users/Leste/Karchin Lab Dropbox/YunZhou Liu/PanIN-2023/PICTograph Mutation Calls 1.31.23/", full.names = T)
file_list <- lapply(files, function(x) read_excel(x)%>%
                      select(contains(c("Hugo_Symbol","HGVSp_Short")))%>%
                      mutate(file = gsub(".*31.23/(.*) Filter.*","\\1",x)))


## excels has duplicated columns, and for AB_14_1.3&1.4 two columns of HGVSp is not the same

ff <- lapply(1:length(file_list), function(x) file_list[[x]][!duplicated(as.list(file_list[[x]]))]%>%
                      select(c(1:2,"file"))%>%
                      rename("Hugo_Symbol" = grep("Hugo",colnames(.)),
                             "HGVSp_Short" = grep("HGVS", colnames(.))))%>%
  do.call(rbind,.)%>%
  mutate(gene = paste0(Hugo_Symbol,HGVSp_Short),
         reigon = gsub("(.*)\\..*","\\1",file),
         reigon = gsub(" ","_",reigon))%>%
  select(gene, file, reigon)
```

## Including Plots
```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
reigon <- unique(ff$reigon)
# remove TC98_2 bc 98_2.5 has duplicated gene name
#reigon <- reigon[-12]

bind_reigon <- function(rei, dat){

  out <- dat%>%
    filter(reigon == rei)%>%
    mutate(type = row_number())%>%
    spread(key = file, value = gene)%>%
    select(-c("type","reigon"))%>%
    as.list()
  return(out)
}

dat_in <- lapply(reigon, function(x) bind_reigon(x,ff))
```

# UPSETr
```{r}
upset(fromList(dat_in[[3]]))
  
```
